name: Workbench
description: A placed or discovered station grants access to recipes, queues, upgrades, or crafting rules tied to its tier and location.
design_notes: A workbench is an access validator, not just a menu opener. The station needs crafting_station_id, tier, proximity or ownership checks, recipe availability, queue state, fuel or tool requirements, and multiplayer access rules so the UI never offers crafts that the station cannot actually complete.
design_purpose: Create spatial commitment around crafting by tying advanced recipes to specific places or structures.
Give base building and progression clear upgrade targets through station tiers.
Separate field crafting, specialized production, repair, and high-tier crafting into readable roles.
player_fantasy: The player feels they have built or found a capable workspace that expands what they can make.

parameters.name: crafting_station_id
parameters.description: Stable station identifier used for interaction, persistence, queue ownership, and multiplayer permissions.

parameters.name: crafting_station_tier
parameters.description: Station upgrade tier used by recipes, queue slots, repair rules, and UI filtering.

parameters.name: interaction_radius_units
parameters.description: Maximum distance from which a player can open, maintain, or confirm station crafting.

parameters.name: required_tool_id
parameters.description: Tool or attachment required before the station can execute certain recipes.

parameters.name: station_recipe_filter_id
parameters.description: Recipe filter used to decide which recipe_id entries are available at the station.

parameters.name: access_permission_id
parameters.description: Permission rule for opening the station, editing queues, or claiming outputs.

edge_cases: The player moves out of interaction_radius_units while a confirmation dialog or queue edit is open.
A station is upgraded, destroyed, or claimed by another player while a craft is pending.
Recipes are visible because of player unlocks but blocked by station tier, missing tool, or fuel requirements.
A save file restores station queues before the station's base structure or ownership data exists.
common_bugs: UI caches a recipe list from the previous station and allows invalid crafting at a lower-tier workbench.
Queue outputs are claimable by players without access_permission_id rights.
Station upgrades change tier but do not migrate existing queue entries or recipe filters.
Interaction prompts remain active after the station is destroyed, moved, or unloaded.
balancing_notes: Station tiers should unlock distinct capabilities, not only small percentage improvements.
Portable stations reduce friction but can weaken exploration, base defense, and settlement progression.
Shared stations need ownership rules that support cooperation without enabling easy griefing.
accessibility_notes: Show station tier, blocked requirements, and access permissions in text within the crafting UI.
Keep station interaction prompts stable and remappable for players using controller or assistive input.

implementation_notes.unity: Model each workbench as a station component with crafting_station_id, tier, recipe filter, queue reference, and permission data. Interaction should revalidate proximity, station existence, tier, and access before every confirm or queue mutation, not only when opening the UI.
implementation_notes.godot: Use a station node with exported Resource references for recipe filters and tier rules. The UI should query station state through a service, and save/load should restore station id, tier, ownership, fuel, and queue entries in a deterministic order.
implementation_notes.unreal: An Actor with replicated station component can own recipe filters, queue entries, and permissions. Server RPCs should validate access_permission_id, player proximity, required tools, and station tier before accepting craft, cancel, or claim actions.
implementation_notes.web: Keep station state as data keyed by crafting_station_id, even if the station is rendered as a sprite or DOM panel. Confirm actions should include station id and expected version so stale UI cannot mutate a moved or upgraded station.

example_games.title: Terraria
example_games.note: Uses crafting stations to unlock different recipe sets.

example_games.title: Valheim
example_games.note: Uses upgradeable workbenches and stations as base progression anchors.
